projectName: Capacium Core V2 — MCP-Server, Exchange, Crawler

execution_recommendation.reason: 30 tasks, 8+ hours total, complex cross-module dependencies, new subsystems, schema migrations, 3 workstreams converging on shared domain model

tasks.title: Extend Kind enum with MCP_SERVER
tasks.description: Add MCP_SERVER = 'mcp-server' to the Kind enum in models.py. Update any Kind-validation code in manifest.py and registry.py to accept the new value. Ensure backward compatibility with existing 6 kinds.
acceptanceCriteria: Kind.MCP_SERVER exists with value 'mcp-server'
Manifest with kind: mcp-server parses without error
Registry accepts and stores capabilities with kind='mcp-server'
Existing 213 tests pass unchanged
cap search --kind mcp-server returns only MCP-server capabilities

tasks.title: Add TrustState enum to models.py
tasks.description: Create TrustState enum with values: discovered, indexed, claimed, verified, audited. Add to models.py alongside Kind.
acceptanceCriteria: TrustState enum exists with 5 values
TrustState values serialize to/from strings correctly
Enum is importable from capacium.models

tasks.title: Create exchange/models.py with core dataclasses
tasks.description: Create the exchange/ package with models.py containing: Listing, MCPMetadata, Publisher, Category, Subcategory, TagCount, Collection, ClaimRequest, VerificationRecord, TrustTransition, SearchQuery dataclasses. All with to_dict/from_dict serialization.
acceptanceCriteria: All 10+ dataclasses defined with type annotations
to_dict() and from_dict() work for all entities
MCPMetadata is nullable on Listing (only for mcp-server type)
SearchQuery supports all 15+ facets
Unit tests for serialization round-trips pass

tasks.title: SQLite schema migration for V2 tables
tasks.description: Create migrations/ package with v2_exchange_crawler.py. Implement schema creation for: listings, publishers, categories, subcategories, tag_counts, collections, crawl_sources, crawl_jobs, crawl_findings, claim_requests, verification_records. All with proper indexes. Migration must be idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS).
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 new tables created on fresh DB
Migration runs idempotently (no error on second run)
All indexes created (≥12 indexes)
Existing capabilities/bundle_members/signatures tables untouched
Foreign keys reference correct tables
Migration runs automatically on Registry init

tasks.title: Seed V1 taxonomy data
tasks.description: Create a taxonomy seeder that inserts the 10 V1 top-level categories and initial subcategories into the categories and subcategories tables. Run as part of migration if tables are empty.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 categories inserted with correct slugs and display names
≥10 subcategories inserted for key categories
Seeder is idempotent (INSERT OR IGNORE)
Categories retrievable via SQL query
Sort order is defined

tasks.title: Implement exchange/listing.py — Listing CRUD
tasks.description: Create listing.py in exchange/ with ListingStore class. Methods: create_listing, get_listing, get_listing_by_name, update_listing, delete_listing, list_listings. All operations go through SQLite via the registry connection pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Create listing with all mandatory fields succeeds
Get listing by ID returns correct data
Get listing by canonical_name returns correct data
Update listing modifies updated_at timestamp
Delete listing removes from DB
list_listings returns all listings with pagination
MCPMetadata stored/retrieved as JSON blob correctly

tasks.title: Implement exchange/trust.py — TrustState machine
tasks.description: Create trust.py with TrustMachine class. Enforce valid state transitions (discovered→indexed→claimed→verified→audited, plus demotion paths). Record all transitions in trust_history. Validate entry criteria per state.
acceptanceCriteria: Forward transitions succeed (5 transitions)
Invalid transitions raise TrustTransitionError
Demotion paths work (verified→claimed, audited→verified)
Each transition recorded in trust_history with timestamp and reason
Entry criteria checked: indexed requires all mandatory fields
Entry criteria checked: claimed requires publisher_id
Unit tests cover all valid and invalid transitions

tasks.title: Implement exchange/taxonomy.py — Category/Tag management
tasks.description: Create taxonomy.py with TaxonomyStore class. Methods: list_categories, get_category, list_subcategories, add_tag, remove_tag, list_tags_by_count, increment_tag_count, decrement_tag_count.
acceptanceCriteria: list_categories returns all seeded categories
get_category returns category with its subcategories
Tag count incremented when listing gets new tag
Tag count decremented when listing loses tag
list_tags_by_count returns tags sorted by popularity
Subcategories correctly linked to parent categories

tasks.title: Implement exchange/search.py — Faceted search engine
tasks.description: Create search.py with ExchangeSearch class. Accept SearchQuery, build dynamic SQL WHERE clauses for all facets. Support full-text search on name+description, type/category/trust/tag/publisher/mcp-client filtering, sorting, pagination.
acceptanceCriteria: Text search matches on canonical_name and short_description
Filter by package_type returns only matching types
Filter by category returns correct listings
Filter by trust_state returns correct listings
Filter by min_trust_state uses state ordering
Filter by tag uses JSON array contains
Filter by mcp_client searches within mcp_metadata JSON
Multiple filters combine with AND semantics
Pagination (limit/offset) works correctly
Sorting by relevance, name, trust, updated works
Performance: <100ms for 1000 listings with 3 filters

tasks.title: Implement exchange/publisher.py — Publisher + Claim logic
tasks.description: Create publisher.py with PublisherStore class and claim/verify workflow. Methods: create_publisher, get_publisher, submit_claim, get_claim, verify_claim (check repo-file or DNS proof), approve_claim, reject_claim.
acceptanceCriteria: Publisher created with slug, display_name, proof
submit_claim creates ClaimRequest in pending state
verify_claim checks repo-file proof (mock HTTP)
Approved claim transitions listing from indexed to claimed
Verified claim transitions listing from claimed to verified
Rejected claim stays in current state
Publisher.verified updates on successful verification

tasks.title: Implement exchange/collection.py — Collection management
tasks.description: Create collection.py with CollectionStore class. Methods: create_collection, get_collection, add_listing_to_collection, remove_listing_from_collection, list_collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Collection created with slug and display_name
Listings added/removed from collection
Get collection returns ordered listing IDs
list_collections returns all collections

tasks.title: Extend cap search with type/category/trust/tag filters
tasks.description: Add --type, --category, --trust, --min-trust, --tag, --mcp-client, --publisher, --sort, --json flags to the existing cap search command. When searching the local exchange index (not just installed capabilities), use ExchangeSearch.
acceptanceCriteria: cap search --type mcp-server returns only MCP listings
cap search --category developer-tools filters by category
cap search --trust verified filters by trust state
cap search --tag filesystem filters by tag
cap search --mcp-client cursor filters MCP by client
cap search --json outputs JSON array
Multiple filters can be combined
Backward compatible: cap search <query> without flags still works

tasks.title: Implement cap info command
tasks.description: Create commands/info.py. Accepts capability name, looks up in listings table (or falls back to capabilities table for installed-only). Displays full metadata including trust state, publisher, MCP details, install status.
acceptanceCriteria: cap info <name> displays all listing fields
MCP-specific fields shown only for mcp-server type
Trust state shown with visual indicator (✓ for verified+)
Publisher shown with verification status
Falls back to installed capability info if no listing exists
cap info nonexistent shows 'not found' error

tasks.title: Implement cap claim command
tasks.description: Create commands/claim.py. Submits a claim request for a listing. Creates publisher if needed. Generates verification token and instructs user to place file/DNS record.
acceptanceCriteria: cap claim <name> prompts for publisher info if not existing
Generates unique verification token
Instructs user on repo-file or DNS proof method
Creates ClaimRequest in pending state
cap claim <name> --verify triggers proof validation

tasks.title: Implement cap exchange subcommands
tasks.description: Create commands/exchange.py with subcommands: cap exchange search (alias for enhanced search), cap exchange categories (list taxonomy), cap exchange tags (list popular tags).
acceptanceCriteria: cap exchange search works like enhanced cap search
cap exchange categories lists all 10 categories with subcategories
cap exchange tags lists top-N tags with counts
cap exchange tags --limit 20 limits output

tasks.title: Implement cap trust promote/history commands
tasks.description: Create commands/trust.py with: cap trust promote <name> <state> (admin operation to advance trust), cap trust history <name> (show trust transitions).
acceptanceCriteria: cap trust promote advances trust state with validation
cap trust promote with invalid transition shows error
cap trust history shows all transitions with timestamps
Operations require --admin flag or env var for safety

tasks.title: Create crawler/models.py with crawler dataclasses
tasks.description: Create the crawler/ package with models.py containing: CrawlSource, CrawlJob, CrawlFinding dataclasses with serialization.
acceptanceCriteria: All 3 dataclasses defined with type annotations
to_dict/from_dict serialization works
CrawlFinding.status supports: raw, normalized, duplicate, promoted, rejected
CrawlJob.status supports: pending, running, completed, failed

tasks.title: Implement crawler/engine.py — Crawl orchestrator
tasks.description: Create engine.py with CrawlEngine class. Methods: run_crawl(source_id), run_all_sources(), get_job_status(job_id). Orchestrates: fetch → normalize → classify → dedup → create findings.
acceptanceCriteria: run_crawl creates a CrawlJob and runs pipeline
Job status updates through pending → running → completed/failed
Findings count accurately tracked
Errors captured in job.errors list
run_all_sources processes enabled sources sequentially

tasks.title: Implement crawler/sources/github.py — GitHub source
tasks.description: Create GitHub source fetcher. Uses GitHub Search API (stdlib urllib) to discover repos by topic, keyword, and language. Extracts repo metadata: name, description, owner, topics, language, stars, license.
acceptanceCriteria: Searches GitHub API with configurable query
Handles rate limiting with backoff
Extracts: name, description, owner, topics, language, stars, license, default_branch
Respects source config for search terms
Works without GITHUB_TOKEN (public search), faster with token
Returns list of raw metadata dicts

tasks.title: Implement crawler/normalizer.py + classifier.py
tasks.description: Create normalizer (standardizes metadata fields, cleans text, extracts structured data) and classifier (infers package_type from signals, assigns primary_category and tags).
acceptanceCriteria: Normalizer standardizes names to kebab-case
Normalizer truncates descriptions to 280 chars
Normalizer extracts license from SPDX identifiers
Classifier infers mcp-server from repo topics/name containing 'mcp'
Classifier infers skill from SKILL.md presence
Classifier assigns category from keyword matching
Classifier suggests tags from repo topics and keywords

tasks.title: Implement crawler/dedup.py — Deduplication engine
tasks.description: Create dedup.py with Deduplicator class. Implements: URL canonicalization (normalize GH URLs), name matching (fuzzy), canonical source selection, alias linking.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub URL variants normalized to canonical form
github.com/user/repo.git → github.com/user/repo
github.com/user/repo/tree/main → github.com/user/repo
Duplicate findings linked to existing listing via canonical_match_id
similarity_score computed for name matches
Source precedence: capability.yaml > primary org > most stars

tasks.title: Implement crawler/claim_prep.py — Claim/verify preparation
tasks.description: Create claim_prep.py with ClaimPreparator class. Detects potential owners from GitHub repo metadata, extracts contact hints (homepage URL, profile URL, email from commits), flags high-confidence claim candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: Owner detection extracts GitHub org/user as detected_owner
Contact hints extracted from repo homepage, owner profile
Claim candidates flagged when owner confidence is high
Results stored on CrawlFinding.detected_owner and contact_hints

tasks.title: Implement cap crawl subcommands
tasks.description: Create commands/crawl.py with: cap crawl run [source], cap crawl sources (list/add), cap crawl findings [job-id], cap crawl promote <finding-id>.
acceptanceCriteria: cap crawl run triggers crawl for specified source
cap crawl run --all triggers crawl for all enabled sources
cap crawl sources lists configured sources
cap crawl findings lists findings for a job with status
cap crawl promote creates listing from finding

tasks.title: Add /listings endpoint to registry server
tasks.description: Extend registry_server.py with GET /v1/listings endpoint. Accepts all SearchQuery parameters. Returns paginated listing results with full metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/listings returns all listings
Query parameters filter results (type, category, trust, tag, etc.)
Pagination via limit/offset works
Response format matches CapabilityListResponse pattern
MCP metadata included for mcp-server listings

tasks.title: Add /categories, /tags, /publishers API endpoints
tasks.description: Extend registry_server.py with taxonomy and publisher endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/categories returns all categories with subcategories
GET /v1/tags returns tags with counts, sortable
GET /v1/publishers returns publisher list
GET /v1/publishers/{slug} returns publisher detail

tasks.title: Add /claims and /crawl API endpoints
tasks.description: Extend registry_server.py with claim submission and crawl management endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/claims creates claim request
GET /v1/claims/{id} returns claim status
POST /v1/crawl/jobs starts crawl job
GET /v1/crawl/jobs/{id} returns job with findings summary
POST /v1/crawl/findings/{id}/promote promotes finding

tasks.title: Update OpenAPI spec for V2 endpoints
tasks.description: Extend specs/registry-openapi.yaml with all new endpoints, schemas (Listing, MCPMetadata, Publisher, Category, SearchQuery, CrawlJob, ClaimRequest), and updated Kind enum.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenAPI spec includes all new endpoints
Listing schema includes all mandatory and optional fields
MCPMetadata schema defined
Kind enum includes mcp-server
Spec validates without errors

tasks.title: MCP manifest validation and mcp_metadata extraction
tasks.description: Extend manifest.py to validate mcp-server manifests and extract MCPMetadata from the 'mcp' section of capability.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: Manifest with kind=mcp-server and mcp section parses to MCPMetadata
mcp-server without mcp section generates validation warning
supported_clients, transport, runtime fields extracted
Unknown mcp fields are ignored (forward compatibility)
Non-mcp-server kinds ignore mcp section

tasks.title: Unit tests for exchange models and trust machine
tasks.description: Write comprehensive tests for all exchange dataclasses, serialization, and TrustState machine transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: All dataclass to_dict/from_dict round-trips tested
All valid trust transitions tested
All invalid trust transitions tested (expect error)
MCPMetadata null handling tested
SearchQuery construction tested
≥95% coverage for exchange/models.py and exchange/trust.py

tasks.title: Integration tests for listing CRUD, search, and crawl pipeline
tasks.description: Write integration tests that exercise: listing creation → search → crawl finding → promote → claim → verify flow end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Listing CRUD integration test with real SQLite
Faceted search with 5+ filters test
Crawl pipeline integration with mock GitHub API
Finding promotion to listing test
Claim → verify flow test
Schema migration idempotency test
Regression: all 213 existing tests still pass

tasks.title: Update documentation and agents.md for V2
tasks.description: Update docs/cli-reference.md with new commands, docs/registry.md with new endpoints, docs/manifest.md with mcp section. Update agents.md with V2 architecture, new module layout, and conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI reference covers all new commands
Registry docs cover all new API endpoints
Manifest docs cover mcp section
agents.md reflects V2 module architecture
README.md updated with V2 feature highlights
